summary: Analyzed the collected primary/official sources into a provider-neutral risk model, a concrete safe web-to-Markdown capture workflow, and a file-by-file map for the requested public example package.
next_angle: Synthesize the five requested English Markdown files under the public example package shape, using the workflow map as the scaffold and preserving provider-neutral language.
open_questions: No blocking research questions remain; synthesis should still verify that generated package text avoids private paths, local tool names, chat IDs, tokens, and real unsanitized capture transcripts.

sources.title: RFC 9309: Robots Exclusion Protocol
sources.note: Used to frame robots.txt as crawler coordination/politeness, not authorization.

sources.title: RFC 9110: HTTP Semantics
sources.note: Used for safe-method framing and redirect Location handling.

sources.title: Python urllib.parse documentation
sources.note: Used for the warning that URL parsing APIs do not validate security-sensitive input.

sources.title: OWASP Server-Side Request Forgery Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Used for URL-controlled fetch risks, parser abuse, and allowlist/network containment guidance.

sources.title: OWASP LLM Prompt Injection Prevention Cheat Sheet
sources.note: Used for untrusted fetched-context handling, action screening, and quarantine/privilege separation patterns.

sources.title: CommonMark Spec 0.31.2
sources.note: Used to support the caveat that Markdown can include raw HTML blocks and should not be treated as sanitized output.

sources.title: Microsoft MarkItDown repository
sources.note: Used only as an illustrative converter reference while keeping the example converter-neutral.

findings.text: The example should organize risks into five reader-facing categories: permission/politeness, request safety, URL and network containment, conversion/Markdown handling, and prompt-injection boundaries.

A safe capture workflow can be presented as six stages: intake, preflight, fetch, convert, analyze, and package. The workflow should require URL policy checks before fetch, redirect revalidation, bounded content, provenance metadata, and untrusted-content handling during analysis.

The five requested files map cleanly to package roles: README.md for purpose and safety boundaries, prompt.md for the worker contract, research-plan.md for gates and stages, sources.md for official references, and final-report.md for the evaluated workflow and artifact checklist.

The public example should not include a live capture transcript in the initial five-file package. A transcript is useful only as a later synthetic or explicitly sanitized extension, because the requested deliverable is a package template and should avoid redistribution concerns.

The example should stay converter-neutral. MarkItDown can be named as one illustrative converter with safety caveats, but the workflow should describe hardened fetch and bounded conversion independent of any required dependency.

analysis_artifacts.note: Intermediate synthesis map of risks, workflow stages, resolved open questions, source anchors, and package-file responsibilities.

adequacy.goal_alignment: Adequacy was not formally evaluated in this analyze iteration. The analysis now directly supports the goal by translating collected sources into the requested safe capture workflow and example-package structure.
adequacy.coverage_summary: Covered risk model, workflow stages, file mapping, and both prior open questions. Remaining work is synthesis of the actual five Markdown files followed by a verify phase.

covered_requirements.evidence: workspace/analysis/workflow-map.md defines a converter-neutral workflow and uses MarkItDown only as an illustrative converter reference.
workspace/analysis/workflow-map.md maps README.md, prompt.md, research-plan.md, sources.md, and final-report.md to concrete roles.
The map anchors claims in RFC 9309, RFC 9110, Python docs, OWASP cheat sheets, CommonMark, and the official MarkItDown repository.

coverage_gaps.gap: The actual five Markdown package files have not yet been drafted.
A verify pass has not yet checked the final package for private paths, local tool names, chat IDs, tokens, or unsanitized real captures.

evidence_risks.risk: MarkItDown repository text may change over time; keep any converter-specific language illustrative and avoid depending on exact current implementation details.

adequacy.recommended_next_angle: Draft the five requested Markdown files from the workflow map and source list, then run a verify phase before finalization.
blocking_reasons: Requested package files are not yet produced.

validation_evidence.reason: The analysis explicitly maps evidence to the user's stated goal and deliverable, but this was not a formal verify phase.
Both open questions were resolved for synthesis: omit live transcript initially and keep converter references provider-neutral.
Synthesis is the next useful phase because evidence has been organized and the package structure is now clear.
